heading: Development of definitions for certain terms relating to intelligence
label: 3316c Development of definitions for certain terms relating to intelligence
notes: Requirement to Develop Lexicon for Terms Relating to Gray Zone Activities, (c), , , 1029, provided that:Pub. L. 117–103, div. X, title VIII, § 825(b)(5)136 Stat. 1028Mar. 15, 2022[“(b)National Intelligence Estimate.—]“(5) .—In this subsection [pars. (1) to (4) not classified to the Code]:Definitions“(A) .—The term ‘gray zone activity’ means an activity to advance the national interests of a State that—Gray zone activity“(i) falls between ordinary statecraft and open warfare;“(ii) is carried out with an intent to maximize the advancement of interests of the state without provoking a kinetic military response by the United States; and“(iii) falls on a spectrum that ranges from covert adversary operations, to detectible covert adversary operations, to unattributable adversary operations, to deniable adversary operations, to open adversary operations.“(B) .—The term ‘covert adversary operation’ means an operation by an adversary that—Covert adversary operation“(i) the adversary intends to remain below the threshold at which the United States detects the operation; and“(ii) does stay below such threshold.“(C) .—The term ‘detectible covert adversary operation’ means an operation by an adversary that—Detectible covert adversary operation“(i) the adversary intends to remain below the threshold at which the United States detects the operation; but“(ii) is ultimately detected by the United States at a level below the level at which the United States will publicly attribute the operation to the adversary.“(D) .—The term ‘unattributable adversary operation’ means an operation by an adversary that the adversary intends to be detected by the United States, but remains below the threshold at which the United States will publicly attribute the operation to the adversary.Unattributable adversary operation“(E) .—The term ‘deniable adversary operation’ means an operation by an adversary that—Deniable adversary operation“(i) the adversary intends to be detected and publicly or privately attributed by the United States; and“(ii) the adversary intends to deny, to limit the response by the United States, and any allies of the United States.“(F) .—The term ‘open adversary operation’ means an operation by an adversary that the adversary openly acknowledges as attributable to the adversary.Open adversary operation“(c)Requirement to Develop Lexicon.—“(1) .—The Director of National Intelligence, acting through the National Intelligence Council, shall develop a lexicon of common terms (and corresponding definitions for such terms) for concepts associated with gray zone activities.Requirement“(2) .—In developing the lexicon under paragraph (1), the National Intelligence Council shall include in the lexicon each term (and the corresponding definition for each term) specified in subsection (b)(5), unless the National Intelligence Council determines that an alternative term (or alternative definition)—Considerations“(A) more accurately describes a concept associated with gray zone activities; or“(B) is preferable for any other reason.“(3)Report.—“(A) .—The Director of National Intelligence shall publish a report containing the lexicon developed under paragraph (1).Publication“(B) .—The report under subparagraph (A) shall be published in unclassified form.”Form

DefinitionsFor definitions of “congressional intelligence committees” and “intelligence community” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of div. X of Pub. L. 117–103section 3003 of this title

or“(B) is preferable for any other reason.“(3)Report.—“(A) .—The Director of National Intelligence shall publish a report containing the lexicon developed under paragraph (1).Publication“(B) .—The report under subparagraph (A) shall be published in unclassified form.”Form

children.text: Such others terms as may be jointly determined necessary by the Director of National Intelligence and the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security.

children.heading: Application to activities of intelligence community
children.label: b Application to activities of intelligence community
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence shall ensure that the definitions developed under subsection (a) are used uniformly across activities of the intelligence community with respect to the corresponding terms specified in such subsection.

children.heading: Notice of modifications
children.label: c Notice of modifications
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence and the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees notification of any modification by the Director and Under Secretary to a definition of a term specified in subsection (a) following the initial publication of the definition under such subsection.
the Committees on Armed Services of the House of Representatives and the Senate.
